Exempt from Visitor (V) visa for short stay under Agreement on Mutual Visa Exemption for Short-Term Stays. Agreement signed April 9, 2025 (Moscow); entered into force December 19, 2025 after parliamentary ratification by both states. 90 days within a 180-day period. Previously only diplomatic/official/service passports were covered. Ordinary passport visa-free access confirmed effective December 19, 2025.

HKSAR passport holders exempt from Visitor (V) visa for short-term stays and airport transit; 90 days within a rolling 12-month period. Change published by Colombian consulate January 30, 2026, with immediate effect, based on a 2023 Foreign Ministry resolution now operationalised. Prior record listed HKG only in a vague 'special territories of PRC' catch-all entry.

visa_policy.notes: Colombia exempts nationals of approximately 100+ countries/territories from the Visitor (V) visa for short stays under 90 days under Resolution 5488 of 2022 and subsequent amendments. Colombia does NOT offer visa-on-arrival. Visa regime does not foresee 'visa on arrival' per official documentation. Key 2025–2026 changes: (1) Israel removed from visa-free list effective June 5, 2025 by Resolution 8558 of 2025 (reciprocal action following Israel's visa requirement for Colombians). (2) Belarus added to ordinary passport visa-free list effective December 19, 2025 - agreement signed April 9, 2025, entered into force after parliamentary ratification by both states on December 19, 2025 (previously only diplomatic/service passports were covered). (3) Mongolia added to ordinary passport visa-free list effective January 26, 2026 (previously only diplomatic/official passports were covered). (4) Hong Kong SAR now has an explicit separate visa-free entry (effective January 30, 2026). (5) Taiwan ordinary passport holders require a visa unless holding a qualifying US or Schengen credential. Nicaraguan nationals are visa-exempt but pay a USD 10 fee; Canadian nationals pay CAD 85 immigration control fee per Resolution 2676 of 2023. CAN and MERCOSUR nationals may use their national ID document as travel document for tourist short stays.

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Nicaragua, Myanmar, People's Republic of China, Taiwan and Vietnam who prove at the time of arrival in Colombia that they have a permanent residence permit issued by the United States are exempt from the Visitor (V) visa for stays up to 90 days. Note: Taiwan was removed from the ordinary visa-free list in May 2023 but retains conditional access via qualifying credentials.

credential.subtype: any valid visa (valid for more than 6 months from arrival date; C1 transit visa excluded)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Nicaragua, Myanmar, People's Republic of China, Taiwan, Thailand and Vietnam who prove at the time of arrival in Colombia that they hold a visa valid for more than six months issued by the United States are exempt from the Visitor (V) visa for stays up to 90 days. US C1 transit visas do not qualify. Note: Taiwan ordinary passport holders require this credential following removal from the unconditional visa-free list in May 2023.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa (valid for more than 6 months from arrival date)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Nicaragua, Myanmar, People's Republic of China, Taiwan, Thailand and Vietnam who prove at the time of arrival in Colombia that they hold a visa valid for more than six months issued by any country belonging to the Schengen Area are exempt from the Visitor (V) visa for stays up to 90 days.

credential.subtype: permanent resident card (Chile, Mexico, or Peru)

conditional_access.conditions: Foreigners of any nationality who prove permanent residence in any member country of the Alianza del Pacífico (Chile, Mexico, and Peru) are exempt from the Colombia Visitor (V) visa regardless of their nationality. Activities must not generate payment for services or salary in Colombia.

credential.subtype: permanent resident card (Bolivia, Ecuador, or Peru)

conditional_access.conditions: Foreigners of any nationality who prove permanent residence in any member country of the Comunidad Andina de Naciones (Bolivia, Ecuador, and Peru) are exempt from the Colombia Visitor (V) visa regardless of their nationality.
Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2013. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Algeria may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa for diplomatic/official/service passports signed in 2018. Note: as of December 19, 2025, ordinary passport holders of Belarus also have full visa-free access under the Agreement on Mutual Visa Exemption for Short-Term Stays (signed April 9, 2025; entered into force December 19, 2025 after parliamentary ratification). This entry retains the original diplomatic/service exemption record for completeness.
Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2017. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Cambodia may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 30 days.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 1987. Only holders of Diplomatic passports of China (PRC) may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 30 days. Official and Service passport holders are NOT covered by this agreement.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 1994. Holders of Diplomatic passports of Cuba are exempt from visas for an indefinite period (as long as documents are valid). Holders of Official passports of Cuba are visa-exempt for up to 6 months. Service passport holders are NOT covered.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2015. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Egypt may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days within a 6-month term.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2001. Holders of Diplomatic and Official passports of India may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days, which may be extended at the request of the Mission or Consulate to which the holder is attached. Service passport holders are NOT covered.
Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 1998. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Iran may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 30 days.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2015. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Lebanon may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days during a period of 180 days.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa for diplomatic/official passports signed in 2013. Note: as of January 26, 2026, ordinary passport holders of Mongolia also have full visa-free access under a new agreement (see visa_free list). This entry retains the original diplomatic/official exemption record for completeness.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2017. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Myanmar may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days during a period of 180 days.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2017. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Nicaragua may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days. Note: ordinary passport holders are also visa-exempt but subject to a USD 10 fee per Resolution 2676 of 2023.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2024. Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service passports of Palestine may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days during a period of 180 days.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2013. Holders of Diplomatic and Official passports of Thailand may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days. Service passport holders are NOT covered.

Bilateral Agreement on Exemption Visitor Visa signed in 2012. Holders of Diplomatic and Official passports of Vietnam may enter Colombia without a visitor visa for up to 90 days. Service passport holders are NOT covered.

cbi.notes: Colombia does not offer a direct citizenship-by-investment program. Dual citizenship is recognized under Colombian law (a person does not have to renounce their previous nationality to acquire Colombian citizenship). The path from investment to citizenship involves: obtaining an M (Migrant) Investor Visa, holding it for at least 3-5 continuous years to qualify for permanent residency (R Visa), then after 5 continuous years of domicile under R Visa, applying for naturalization - a total estimated 8-10 year process. This is naturalization via residency, not a CBI program. Dual citizenship confirmed via Cancillería official sources.

programs.name: Migrant Investor Visa (M) - Real Estate Investment
programs.notes: Migrant Visa (M) for foreigners making direct foreign investment in real estate. Minimum investment: 350 SMMLV (Colombia's legal monthly minimum wage; approx. COP 612,816,750 / USD 161,300 at 2026 SMMLV of COP 1,750,905). Visa valid up to 3 years, multiple entry. Automatically loses validity if holder remains outside Colombia more than 180 consecutive days. After holding M visa(s) for at least 3 continuous years (per official search result citing Resolución 5477 criteria), may qualify for R (Resident) Visa. Regulated by Resolución 5477 of July 22, 2022.

programs.name: Migrant Investor Visa (M) - Direct Foreign Investment in Company
programs.notes: Migrant Visa (M) for foreigners investing in a Colombian company. Minimum investment: 100 SMMLV. For large-scale direct foreign investment, minimum is 650 SMMLV. Regulated by Resolución 5477 of July 22, 2022.

programs.name: Resident Investor Visa (R) - Direct Foreign Investment
programs.notes: Resident Visa (R) granted directly to foreigners making a direct foreign investment of no less than USD 100,000 in their own name, in accordance with the International Investments Statute. This grants immediate permanent residency (R Visa). R Visa must be renewed every 5 years. Regulated by Resolución 5477 of July 22, 2022. Source: official Cancillería Resident Investor's Visa page.

programs.name: Qualified Resident Visa (R) - Accumulated Time
programs.notes: A foreigner who has held a temporary/migrant visa and lived in Colombia regularly for at least 5 continuous uninterrupted years may apply for the Qualified Resident's Visa (R). Application must be made within at least 30 calendar days prior to visa expiration. R Visa renewed every 5 years.

programs.name: Digital Nomad / Remote Worker Visa (V - Nómada Digital)
programs.eligibility: Foreigners who provide remote work or telecommuting services exclusively for foreign companies (as independent contractor or employee), or who start a digital content or IT venture of interest to Colombia. Must hold passport from a visa-exempt nationality (per Resolution 5488 of 2022) or apply for a V visa if nationality is not exempt. Minimum income: 3x Colombia's SMMLV per month (approx. COP 5,252,715 / USD 1,400 at 2026 rates). Must have health insurance covering Colombia.
programs.processing_time: Officially up to 30 calendar days; in practice 10-15 business days
programs.notes: Created under Resolución 5477 of 2022. Visa-exempt nationalities can enter without a visa and stay under Entry Permission for up to 90 days for digital nomad activities. For longer stays, a Visitor (V) visa for digital nomads can be issued for up to 2 years. Income requirement is indexed annually to Colombia's SMMLV. Official Cancillería page: https://www.cancilleria.gov.co/v/nomadadigital and Migración Colombia: https://www.migracioncolombia.gov.co/es/informacion-para-extranjeros/visas/visas-para-nomadas-digitales

agreements: Andean Community (CAN) - free movement of persons; CAN nationals (Bolivia, Ecuador, Peru, and Colombia) may enter for short tourist stays using national ID document; Colombia is a founding member

MERCOSUR - associated state; CAN/MERCOSUR nationals exempt from passport requirement for tourist short stays using national ID; Colombia acceded to MERCOSUR Travel Documents Agreement (deposit March 2026)

Pacific Alliance (Alianza del Pacífico) - Colombia is a founding member (with Chile, Mexico, Peru); free mobility of persons pillar; permanent residents of Pacific Alliance countries are exempt from Colombia visitor visa regardless of nationality

outbound_official.notes: Official source is the Colombian Cancillería (Ministry of Foreign Affairs) PDF titled 'Lista-Paises-exigen-visa-mayo-2026', hosted at cancilleria.gov.co/sites/default/files/FOTOS2025/. The server returns HTTP 403 for direct fetch; URL confirmed via Google search index. The document is the official 'Cuadro de Estados y Territorios que exigen o NO visas a los colombianos' (Table of States and Territories that do or do not require visas for Colombians), updated May 2026. The canonical FAQ page linking this document is https://www.cancilleria.gov.co/faq/quienes-deben-tramitar-la-visa-colombiana. Colombia does not have a visa-on-arrival or government-run e-visa scheme for outbound travel. Canada and UK require prior electronic authorization (eTA/ETA) but no consular visa - classified as ETA. EU ETIAS (for Schengen) is upcoming but not yet enforced as of May 2026. Israel was removed from the visa-free list effective June 5, 2025 (Resolution 8558 of 2025). South American CAN/MERCOSUR nations (Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Ecuador, Paraguay, Peru, Uruguay) allow entry with national ID only.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Tourism (V-Tourism)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay entry for leisure, cultural activities, and sightseeing in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) visa category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Nationals from ~100+ countries are visa-exempt for up to 90 days (180 days per calendar year) and do not need this visa. Required by nationalities not on the visa-free list. Valid up to 2 years; cannot exceed 180 days total per calendar year inside Colombia. All visas are electronic (no passport stamp).

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Business (V-Business)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term entry for commercial negotiations, investment planning, business meetings, or attending conferences in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. For visa-exempt nationalities, no visa is needed for business visits under 90 days. For longer business presence with capital investment (100× SMMLV in a company), the M (Migrant) Business Visa applies instead.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Worker (M-Worker)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term visa for foreigners with a fixed-term or indefinite employment contract with a Colombian company or entity.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Valid up to 3 years, multiple entry. Automatically loses validity if holder remains outside Colombia for more than 6 consecutive months. After 2-5 years on M visa (depending on subcategory), holder may qualify for R (Resident) visa.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Technical Assistance (V-Technical)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term visa for foreigners providing professional technical assistance or specialized services to Colombian entities.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. For temporary technical work. For long-term professional work, the M-Worker or M-Expertise visa applies.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Expertise/Professional (M-Expertise)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners practicing an independent profession or providing specialized expertise services in Colombia on a long-term basis.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Valid up to 3 years. Requires demonstration of professional expertise. Loses validity if absent from Colombia more than 6 consecutive months.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Student (V-Student)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners enrolled in an educational institution in Colombia, including academic programs and language training.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Valid up to 2 years or for the duration of the academic program. Must maintain enrollment at a Colombian institution. Electronic visa with no passport stamp.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Digital Nomad (V-Nómada Digital)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners who work remotely for foreign companies or clients, or who develop digital content or IT ventures of interest to Colombia, while living in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Created under Resolución 5477 de 2022. For visa-exempt nationalities, no visa is needed for digital nomad activities under 90 days. Requires minimum monthly income of 3× Colombia's SMMLV (~USD 1,400/month at 2026 rates). Must have health insurance covering Colombia. Income requirement indexed annually to SMMLV. Cannot earn income from Colombian sources.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Retirement/Pensioner (M-Pensionado)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners receiving a stable pension or retirement income from a foreign source who wish to reside in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Minimum pension income requirement: 3× Colombia's SMMLV (~COP 5,252,715/month, ~USD 1,400/month at 2026 rates). Pension must be periodic and from a foreign source. After 5 continuous years, holder may apply for R (Resident) visa. Considered one of the more accessible retirement visas in Latin America. FBI background check may be required for US citizens.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Real Estate Investment (M-Real Estate)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners making direct foreign investment in Colombian real estate.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Minimum investment: 350× SMMLV (~COP 612,816,750 / ~USD 161,300 at 2026 rates). After 3 continuous years on M visa, may qualify for R (Resident) visa. Automatically loses validity if holder remains outside Colombia more than 6 consecutive months.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Business/Company Investment (M-Business)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners investing in or owning a share of a Colombian commercial company.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Minimum investment: 100× SMMLV in the company. For large-scale direct foreign investment, minimum is 650× SMMLV. After 3 continuous years on M visa, may qualify for R (Resident) visa.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - Direct Foreign Investment (R-Investor)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency visa granted directly to foreigners making a substantial direct foreign investment in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of R (Resident) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Minimum investment: USD 100,000 (or equivalent in COP) in one's own name per the International Investments Statute. Grants immediate permanent residency - no need to first hold an M visa. R Visa must be renewed every 5 years. Expires if absent from Colombia for more than 2 consecutive years.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Working Holiday (V-Working Holiday)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for young foreigners from countries with reciprocal working holiday agreements who wish to travel and work temporarily in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Available only to nationals of countries with bilateral working holiday agreements with Colombia. Age restrictions typically apply (18-30 years). Allows temporary employment to fund travel.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Airport Transit (V-Transit)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for passengers transiting through Colombian international airports without entering Colombian territory.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Most passengers may transit without a visa for up to 24 hours. Certain nationalities require a transit visa. Must not leave the international zone of the airport.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Medical Treatment (V-Medical)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners entering Colombia to receive medical treatment, healthcare consultations, or medical procedures.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Required documentation includes proof of medical appointment/treatment in Colombia. Visa-exempt nationalities may enter for medical purposes without a visa for stays under 90 days.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Spouse/Partner of Colombian National (M-Marriage)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreign spouses or permanent partners of Colombian citizens or Colombian residents.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Includes both legally married spouses and permanent/common-law partners. Open work permit included. After 3 continuous years on M-Marriage visa, holder may apply for R (Resident) visa. Automatically loses validity if absent from Colombia more than 6 consecutive months.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Parent/Child of Colombian National (M-Family)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreign parents of Colombian nationals (by birth or adoption) or foreign children adopted by Colombian nationals.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Covers: foreign parent by birth of a Colombian national, and parent/child by adoption relationships. After 3 continuous years, may apply for R (Resident) visa.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - CAN/Andean Migrant (M-Andean)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for nationals of Andean Community (CAN) member states (Bolivia, Ecuador, Peru) seeking to reside long-term in Colombia under the Andean Migratory Statute.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Facilitated pathway for CAN nationals under Decision 545 of the Andean Community on Free Mobility and Residence. Provides simplified documentation requirements and path to residency.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - MERCOSUR Migrant (M-MERCOSUR)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for nationals of MERCOSUR member and associate states seeking to reside long-term in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Facilitated pathway for nationals of Argentina, Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, Chile under MERCOSUR free movement agreements. Colombia is a MERCOSUR associated state.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Income Recipient/Rentista (M-Rentista)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners with a stable periodic income from foreign sources (rental income, dividends, royalties, etc.) who wish to reside in Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. For foreigners with passive income from creditable sources (different from retirement pensions which have M-Pensionado). Income must be provably periodic. After 5 continuous years, may qualify for R (Resident) visa.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Refugee (M-Refugee)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners who have been granted refugee status by Colombia.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Issued after formal recognition of refugee status by Colombian authorities per Law 1997 of 2019 and international refugee conventions. Fee may be waived for humanitarian cases.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - Accumulated Residency (R-Accumulated)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency visa for foreigners who have held a Colombian temporary/migrant visa and lived in Colombia continuously for the required number of years.
visa_types.notes: Part of R (Resident) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Residency accumulation periods vary by M-visa type: 2 years (CAN/MERCOSUR), 3 years (marriage, investment), 5 years (retirement, rentista, expertise). Application must be made at least 30 calendar days before visa expiration. R Visa renewed every 5 years. Expires if absent from Colombia more than 2 consecutive years.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Courtesy Visa (V-Courtesy)
visa_types.purpose: Visa granted to foreign officials, government representatives, and special categories as a diplomatic courtesy.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Granted at the discretion of the Colombian Ministry of Foreign Affairs to accredited foreign officials and special diplomatic categories. Fee typically waived.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Internship (V-Internship)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners undertaking professional training or internship positions in Colombian organizations.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. For formal internship and professional training programs. Must have a sponsoring organization in Colombia.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Social Cooperation/Volunteering (V-Volunteer)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners participating in development, human rights, or humanitarian projects in Colombia on a volunteer basis.
visa_types.notes: Part of V (Visitor) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Requires sponsorship by a registered Colombian NGO or international organization. No salary may be received from Colombian sources.

visa_types.name: Migrant Visa - Internationalization Promotion (M-Internationalization)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for advanced degree holders (master's or doctorate) in fields of national interest who contribute to Colombia's economic and technological development.
visa_types.notes: Part of M (Migrant) category under Resolución 5477 de 2022. Created to attract highly skilled foreigners in priority sectors. Holder must have at least a master's degree. After 3 continuous years, may qualify for R (Resident) visa.

data_quality.validation_notes: Validation performed June 2026 against live sources; re-validated 2026-06-27; re-validated 2026-06-29. Seven material corrections applied vs. original record: (1) REMOVED Israel (ISR) from visa_free - Colombia imposed visa requirement on Israeli nationals by Resolution 8558 of 2025, effective June 5, 2025, in response to Israel's reciprocal visa requirement for Colombians. (2) ADDED Belarus (BLR) to visa_free for ordinary passports - Agreement on Mutual Visa Exemption for Short-Term Stays signed April 9, 2025 (Moscow); entered into force December 19, 2025 after parliamentary ratification by both states; 90 days within a 180-day period; prior record only had diplomatic/service passports covered. NOTE: the effective date was initially recorded as 'June 2025' (30 days after signing) but the agreement required parliamentary ratification; the confirmed entry-into-force date is December 19, 2025 per Wikipedia and Belarusian official sources. (3) ADDED Mongolia (MNG) to visa_free for ordinary passports - Agreement on Visa Exemption for Short Stays for Holders of Ordinary Passports entered into force January 26, 2026; prior record only had diplomatic/official passports covered. (4) REPLACED the vague 'Special territories of the People's Republic of China (Hong Kong SAR / Macau SAR)' catch-all entry with an explicit Hong Kong SAR (HKG) entry in visa_free - effective January 30, 2026, HKSAR passports confirmed visa-free for 90 days within a rolling 12-month period. Macau SAR removed from visa_free catch-all (Macau SAR status is uncertain/requires eVisa per Wikipedia data - moved to gaps). (5) Updated Nicaragua entry in visa_free to use correct iso3 'NIC'. (6) Updated conditional_access entries for Belarus and Mongolia to flag that ordinary passport holders are now fully visa-free. (7) Re-validation 2026-06-27: corrected Belarus ordinary passport visa-free effective date from 'June 2025' to 'December 19, 2025' - agreement required parliamentary ratification before entry into force; all other visa_free entries re-confirmed present in official lists (Bhutan, Fiji, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Azerbaijan all confirmed); no new additions or removals found. (8) Re-validation 2026-06-29: full re-check against Wikipedia Visa_policy_of_Colombia and migracioncolombia.gov.co. All visa_free entries confirmed present in Wikipedia list. Confirmed: China (PRC) ordinary passports STILL require a visa despite Colombia's April 2025 announcement of intent - Chinese Embassy in Colombia confirmed April 2025 that no implementation plan has been set; China NOT added. Malaysia not confirmed in official Colombia sources - not added. Macau SAR confirmed as visa required / eVisa per Wikipedia Macau visa requirements page - remains in gaps. No other additions or removals identified.
gaps: Direct access to cancilleria.gov.co pages returned HTTP 403 for all WebFetch requests; source data relies on UN-mirror PDF (official content on non-official hosting), secondary immigration alert firms (Fragomen, Vialto, EY), and secondary travel aggregators for 2025-2026 changes. All changes are corroborated by multiple independent sources.

Macau SAR (MAC) status is uncertain: Macau Wikipedia entry lists Colombia as 'eVisa' for Macau ordinary passport holders, but the prior record had both HKG and MAC in a single vague visa_free catch-all. The specific resolution or official page confirming Macau SAR status could not be fetched (403). Macau SAR not listed as visa_free pending official confirmation.

Israel removal resolution number: multiple sources cite 'Resolution 8558 of 2025' and effective date June 5, 2025, but direct official text of the resolution was not accessible (403). The resolution number and effective date are confirmed by multiple independent immigration alert sources (Vialto, EY, Fragomen, Colombia One).

Belarus ordinary passport visa waiver effective date: corrected to December 19, 2025 - agreement signed April 9, 2025 required parliamentary ratification before entry into force; December 19, 2025 date confirmed via Wikipedia (Visa_policy_of_Colombia) and secondary sources citing Belarusian official sources. Direct cancilleria.gov.co confirmation inaccessible (403).
Mongolia ordinary passport visa waiver: confirmed as January 26, 2026 from secondary travel sources; direct cancilleria.gov.co confirmation inaccessible.
Hong Kong SAR visa-free operationalisation: confirmed January 30, 2026 from visahq.news and HKTDC Research; underlying 2023 resolution number not provided by sources.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) status: no official Colombia source confirms a conditional entry exemption for Indian OCI cardholders. Not recorded.
UK visa / residence permit as a qualifying credential: the official Cancillería document lists only United States and Schengen Area as qualifying credential issuers. UK is NOT listed. Not recorded.
Kazakhstan diplomatic/service visa waiver status: a December 2019 agreement is mentioned on Wikipedia but not in the official NWDF PDF. Status uncertain - not recorded.
Exact income threshold for digital nomad visa (3x SMMLV) sourced from non-official immigration law firm sites; official Cancillería page returned 403.
Path to citizenship (naturalization years after R visa) not confirmed verbatim from official source - estimated 5 years under R visa per secondary sources citing Colombian nationality law.
No visa-on-arrival or ETA scheme exists for Colombia per official documentation.
Minimum investment for Resident Investor R Visa (USD 100,000) sourced from Cancillería search results snippet - direct page returned 403.

China (CHN) ordinary passport: Colombia announced in April 2025 (via President Petro and Canciller Sarabia) that procedures to lift visa requirement for Chinese nationals had been initiated. As of June 2026, Chinese Embassy in Colombia confirmed no specific implementation plan has been set; Chinese nationals still require a visa. Monitor for future resolution/announcement.

Malaysia (MYS) ordinary passport: embassies.net lists Malaysia as visa-free for Colombia but this source is unreliable (it also incorrectly lists Israel as visa-free). Wikipedia Visa_policy_of_Colombia does NOT list Malaysia. Not added pending official confirmation.
